what_it_does: Defines an SVG icon showing overlapping file/page outlines, styled as a stroked, unfilled 24x24 line icon.
observations: Uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with no explicit width or height.
Draws one rounded rectangle at x=8, y=3, width=11, height=14, rx=1.
Draws a partial rear page outline with path data M5 7 V21 H16.
Sets stroke width to 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Contains no ids, classes, title, desc, or embedded metadata.
standouts: Minimal two-shape construction for an overlapping-files icon.
Color is inherited from current text color through currentColor.
conventions: Follows a 24x24 inline SVG icon pattern.
Uses presentation attributes directly on the svg element.
Uses rounded stroke styling for line-icon consistency.
Omits accessibility labeling within the asset itself.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria-hidden attribute is present, so accessibility semantics must come from usage context.
notes: A compact currentColor line icon for representing copied or duplicated files.
questions: Should accessibility semantics be embedded here or supplied wherever the icon is used?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a files-copy icon, and the SVG content matches by drawing overlapping page outlines.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a small reusable copy/files icon that adapts to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Contributes a compact stroked overlapping-files symbol that a fused system would keep as a reusable currentColor icon asset for copy or duplicate-file actions.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG line icon depicting overlapping file outlines using one rectangle and one partial path.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing file copy or duplicate-files behavior as a reusable design-system icon.
